scopingMetadata.discoverySource: tests/fixtures/example_scope_committed.json (fictional Northwind Supply Co. — committed/T&M mode fixture; illustrative numbers, not a real client)

engagement.name: ERP Remediation, Optimization & Setup
engagement.feeStructure: Time & Materials, monthly invoicing, Net-15, +5% Tech & Admin Fee

deliverables.name: Remediated migration data
deliverables.acceptanceCriteria: Opening balances tie to source within tolerance

deliverables.name: Optimized processes
deliverables.acceptanceCriteria: Documented workflows execute end-to-end in the ERP

deliverables.name: Configured modules + manufacturing
deliverables.acceptanceCriteria: Each module passes a transaction smoke test

risks.name: Migration data quality worse than discovered
risks.mitigation: Discovery contingency block reserved; escalate via change order if exceeded

risks.name: Third-party module configuration dependencies
risks.impact: Phase 3 blocked on third-party module provisioning
risks.mitigation: Confirm module licensing/access before Phase 3 start

risks.name: Scope expansion beyond committed phases
risks.mitigation: Monthly invoicing surfaces burn early; change-order discipline

assumptions: Client provides ERP admin access and module licensing
T&M billing, monthly, Net-15, with a 5% Technology & Admin Fee
Committed hours are an estimate, not a fixed fee; overages are change orders
exclusions: Custom scripting beyond the four named modules
Historical transaction migration beyond opening balances
clientResponsibilities: Designate a data-remediation SME for Phase 1
recommendation: Single SOW, internally sequenced across three phases plus a discovery contingency; T&M with monthly invoicing keeps burn visible while the clean-up phase de-risks the module setup that follows.
